From my end –","body":{"contentType":"html","content":"

Good Morning Roy,

 

I agree that the best approach to maximize container space during loading would be to collect and store all consolidation shipments at your facility and determine the most efficient loading plan on the loading day.

From my end –

  • I will reiterate to all teams that Italian LCL shipments are quoted based on our consolidation service and are subject to availability. This approach supports our efforts to actively develop this lane by offering competitive pricing based on consolidation costs, with the objective of securing additional shipments.

If the consolidation is fully booked, two options can be offered:

  1. Departure on the next consolidation at the same rate
  2. Departure via co-loader service with an updated quote

 

  • I will request additional allocation from CMA and revert with the outcome. If they are unable to provide additional space, I will confirm the target cost and explore whether the additional equipment can be shipped under your contracts.

From your end –

  • Would you be able to provide a sailing schedule detailing Consol #, Warehouse Loading Date, Week #, etc.? I have attached the current format for reference. Your first consolidation will be #9, so we could begin there.
  • Volume verification
  • Volume verification should be completed Wednesday/Thursday prior to the anticipated loading date.
  • If the confirmed volume is less than 50 m?, please notify Angela (and Import Operations Agent, if applicable) and they will advise the next steps (e.g., hold for additional volume vs. proceed as planned).

 

Best Regards,

 

Denise Ho | Directrice Sénior - Senior Director
Transport International - International Transport

1360 Boul. René-Lévesque Ouest, suite 1400, Montréal, Québec H3G 2W4, Canada

T: 514.849.9377 | F: 514.849.9428 | C: 514.971.2252 | www.axxessintl.com

From: Angela Caballero <angela.caballero@axxessintl.com>
Sent: March 10, 2026 8:48 AM
To: Roy Rigamonti <roy@columbiatransport.it>
Cc: Denise Ho <denise.ho@axxessintl.com>; Domenico Testa <domenico.testa@axxessintl.com>; Ocean Columbia Transport S.r.l. <ocean@columbiatransport.it>
Subject: *** ACTION REQUIERED ** Consol Italy / Columbia * Axxess

 

Good day Roy

 

Thank you for your message.

 

I have just requested from Simona the cargo details we have for next consol #10 / week 12 – ETD 23.03

 

I have noticed that the blank sailing CMA had a few weeks ago has had an impact. In addition, the booking error, which was not properly scheduled for week 11, has caused the cargo volume to accumulate, and as a result, we currently do not have space available.

 

We will evaluate the available options and come back to you with our comments shortly.

 

Thank you for sharing your thoughts and for your proactive approach.

 

Have a nice day!

 

Angela Caballero | Superviseure import & export maritime - Ocean Import & Export Supervisor
Transport International - International Transport

1360 Boul. René-Lévesque Ouest, suite 1400, Montréal, Québec H3G 2W4, Canada

T: 514.849.9377 | F: 514.849.9428 | C: 514.708.2232 | www.axxessintl.com

From: Roy Rigamonti <roy@columbiatransport.it>
Sent: March 10, 2026 7:23 AM
To: Angela Caballero <angela.caballero@axxessintl.com>
Cc: Denise Ho <denise.ho@axxessintl.com>; Domenico Testa <domenico.testa@axxessintl.com>; Ocean Columbia Transport S.r.l. <ocean@columbiatransport.it>
Subject: Re: *** ACTION REQUIERED ** 20260219-021: R: Gragnano Trebbiense (PC) - ITALY to Montreal Canada C/ Garant Machinerie PO 2117-1937/ 2031-1893/ 2152-2009 // Columbia ref. 26/300155

 

Dear Angela,

 

I'm writing to you now, though I'm sure we'll have the opportunity to discuss this in person next week regarding the upcoming consolidations.

 

Simona and the team are working to find the best way to optimize our consolidated shipments, and I was wondering if it would be possible to consider moving to 2x40'HC per week starting now. Given the unique characteristics of some products—non-standard dimensions, non-stackable items, etc.—the ideal approach would be to collect and store all cargo at our facility as soon as it's ready. This way, on loading day, we can optimize the container loading to the fullest.

 

Simona mentioned that your pricing team sometimes suggests LCL for convenience, but I'm not sure if this is actually cost-effective for you on the destination side, considering deconsolidation expenses. I presume you have a container allotment with CMA—would it be possible to extend it to two containers? Alternatively, if you have a target cost in mind, it might be helpful if we could source additional containers with another carrier when needed, provided we can match your pricing.

 

Please consider this email as a brainstorming exercise. I've asked my team to accommodate any request you send our way and to view any potential challenges as opportunities to optimize loads, reduce costs, and ultimately give you more flexibility in your sales.

 

Thank you, and have a great day.

 

Best regards, 

 

Roy Rigamonti

Managing Director |  Columbia Transport

roy@columbiatransport.it |  + 39 3492801896

Cc: Simona <simona@columbiatransport.it>; Mirella Baini <mirella@columbiatransport.it>; Sales Columbia Transport S.r.l. <sales@columbiatransport.it>
Subject: Re: *** ACTION REQUIERED ** 20260219-021: R: Gragnano Trebbiense (PC) - ITALY to Montreal Canada C/ Garant Machinerie PO 2117-1937/ 2031-1893/ 2152-2009 // Columbia ref. 26/300155

 

Hello Angela,

 

Thank you for yesterday's meeting. I wanted to recap these instructions for confirmation and clarity, and they will be immediately shared with all colleagues in copy.

 

When we receive a quotation request, it should always be for the weekly consolidated service with the agreed pricing between us. As Denise mentioned yesterday, your pricing team should already have all the elements needed to quote directly. When requests come to us, it's because they require more attention on our side regarding pricing—particularly related to pickup logistics due to non-overlapping locations or special conditions.

 

In the specific case where cargo is more urgent than the consolidated closing or requires a different handling than usual, it's sufficient that this be mentioned in the request sent to us so we can proceed accordingly.

 

Best regards, 

 

Roy Rigamonti

Managing Director |  Columbia Transport

roy@columbiatransport.it |  + 39 3492801896
